analysis.core_story_summary: All the sages listened with rapt attention to the description Lord Shiva made of the Almighty Narayan. But, they had few more queries in store, so they asked Lord Shiva-- "O Lord of Uma! please tell us about the expanse of the Earth- the situation of mountains and the prominent islands.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: References family and marriage roles rooted in traditional contexts.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Bhuvan-Kosh and the Islands — Kids (8-12):** All the sages listened with rapt attention to the description Lord Shiva made of the Almighty Narayan. But, they had few more queries in store, so they asked Lord Shiva-- "O Lord of Uma! please tell us about the expanse of the Earth- the situation of mountains and the prominent islands. This story teaches dedication and sacrifice, respect for nature. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
